content:
Also, Looks like the OCPN plugin manual needs some update..
Some plugins are in main and beta lists.
Some plugins are available in the main catalog but not listed with the "up arrow" (not intuitive since the up arrow is supposed to indicate "included" and not "available" plugins as it says at the beginning).
Some plugins are completely missing.

Plugin list is not up-to-date between the main website and the plugin's manual :
https://opencpn.org/OpenCPN/info/downloadplugins.html
https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/opencpn-plugins/index-main.html
And I believe the list from the website is the most used/ easily accessible for users.

And I believe the list from the website is the most used/ easily accessible for users.

Probably not. The actual list used by users is the catalog, displayed by opencpn under Options | Plugins. The primary website(s) use case is that in this list plugins link to them under "More info"
... that is not to say it should not be corrected. Patches welcome...
3 messages were moved here from #Master > Demo plugin by Alec Leamas.

Probably not. The actual list used by users is the catalog, displayed by opencpn under Options | Plugins.

I meant between the plugins listed in the website and the plugin's manual list.

The webiste is not always maintained, agreed.

Everything else is in git. Note that the actual contents lives in the plugins' repos. The simple way to update this is to use the "Edit this page" link in the manual pages.

If a plugin is missing in the plugins manual please raise an issue at https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/main. For this to be meaningful the plugion should have an updated manual/ directory

If a plugin is missing in the plugins manual please raise an issue at https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/main. For this to be meaningful the plugion should have an updated manual/ directory

Alec, I am ready to revise and improve the TestPlugin Manual , however I do not find it anymore and I don't appear to have access to make those changes.

I do see that you have moved the OpenCPN manual to https://github.com/OpenCPN/OpenCPN/blob/master/manual/modules/ROOT/nav.adoc from the original opencpn-manuals repo and this is what appears here in the manual website .

The manual section I am looking for and cannot find is the Dev Plugin Compile section, it was never located under "Testplugin_pi". It was a part of opencpn-manuals.

I think I found it here https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/ocpn-dev-manual/0.1/index.html and the menus seem to go in circles and I never find the Testplugin Developer documents. I was hopeful to find them from this location, https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/ocpn-dev-manual/0.1/index.html but non of the links lead to that section.

Also I am logged in and there is no Edit button. Why would that be?

Do you know how I can access those pages so that I can edit them?

Thanks
Rick

Ok I found the Plugin Dev Manual, it is in a different repo here, https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/development/tree/main/modules/ROOT/pages

The reason I know that is because of pages like these:
https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/development/blob/main/modules/ROOT/pages/pm-tp-system-structure.adoc
https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/development/blob/main/modules/ROOT/pages/pm-overview-prereq-services.adoc
https://github.com/opencpn-manuals/development/blob/main/modules/ROOT/pages/pm-tp-dev-setup.adoc

Why aren't these pages showing in the menu system? Or at least I can't get there after lots of tries, although there are circular references and not much consistency in the side menu.

Don't we want to have a link to the basics about compiling plugins?

@Alec Leamas Ok now I managed to find a webpage link somehow (don't ask how)

https://opencpn-manuals.github.io/main/ocpn-dev-manual/0.1/pm-tp-template.html

but it does not allow me to edit.
